title: X-Forwarded-For rate limit is trivially bypassable
description: src/server.rs:374-383: handle_inbox uses X-Forwarded-For (first entry) as the rate-limit key. Any client can forge this header, selecting an arbitrary IP to bucket against. An attacker can include X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4 and rotate the last octet to get unlimited inbox requests. Without a trusted proxy stripping/setting this header, the rate limiter provides no real protection. This is a security surface because the inbox accepts Follow activities and triggers outbound HTTP requests (actor fetch + Accept send). Fix: either (a) only trust X-Forwarded-For when behind a known proxy (configured IP range), or (b) use the actual TCP peer address (requires axum ConnectInfo extractor).
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
